title: Himatanthus fallax
taxonomy: Himatanthus fallax is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This genus belongs to the order Gentianales and the family Apocynaceae. Himatanthus fallax is a member of the tribe Plumerieae. The plant is native to South America and is also known as the "Palo Santo". Its classification is further supported by its characteristics, such as its woody stems and clusters of white flowers.
uses: Himatanthus fallax is traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The plant's bark and roots are often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some regions, Himatanthus fallax is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Its medicinal uses are largely based on its bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its safety for human consumption.

constituents: Himatanthus fallax contains active constituents such as alkaloids and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant's alkaloids, particularly the tropane alkaloids, have been identified as the primary active constituents.
